Section 1736t, , , ; , , , provided for short-term export credits.Pub. L. 99–198, title XI, § 112599 Stat. 1482Pub. L. 100–418, title IV, § 4402(a)102 Stat. 1400Dec. 23, 1985Aug. 23, 1988

Export Credit Guarantee Program, , , which stated the sense of Congress that, to the extent that the Commodity Credit Corporation made a specified allocation of credit guarantees available under the export credit guarantee program referred to in section 1736t for short-term credit extended to finance the export sales of United States agricultural commodities and products, such allocation was to be made on a country-only basis and not on a commodity basis or a commodity and country basis, was repealed by , , .Pub.
